events.title: SoH Transits Drop 95% Amid Iran-US Tensions
events.description: Daily vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz have dropped to 0.2m GT, over 95% below normal levels, due to renewed tensions and attacks on vessels in the Middle East Gulf following the breakdown of the Iran-US interim peace deal.

events.title: Ennore Coal Terminal increases max draft
events.description: Effective July 17, 2026, the maximum permissible draft for vessels at Ennore Coal Terminal in India has been increased from 16.00 metres to 18.00 metres.

events.title: Vessel boarded by unauthorized personnel off Yemen
events.location: Gulf of Aden, near Al Mukalla
events.description: A ship was boarded by unauthorized personnel 65 nautical miles south of Al Mukalla, Yemen, in the Gulf of Aden. The UK Maritime Trade Operations warned vessels to transit with caution and report suspicious activity.

events.title: Russia strikes Mykolaiv port in Ukraine
events.description: Russian drones attacked the Ukrainian port of Mykolaiv on July 17, causing further disruption to regional shipping and port operations amid ongoing conflict.

events.title: MSC updates freight rates from South Asia to Europe
events.description: MSC has announced new Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates from India, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, and Bangladesh to Europe, effective 1 August 2026. The rates will apply until further notice, with a maximum validity until 31 August 2026.

events.title: Maersk updates AC1 and AC2 services between Asia and Latin America
events.description: Maersk has updated its AC1 and AC2 services connecting Far East Asia with the West Coast of Latin America as part of its Modular Network strategy to enhance regional connectivity. The changes aim to improve service reliability and efficiency for trade routes between Asia and Latin America.

events.title: Pirates seize small product tanker off Yemen
events.description: A small chemical tanker near Yemen was seized by up to seven armed pirates after issuing a distress call on July 17. The incident poses a risk to maritime security in the region.

events.title: IAG Cargo increases UK-India freight flights
events.description: IAG Cargo will launch a third daily freight service between London Heathrow and Delhi starting 19 September 2026, expanding its UK-India network to 70 weekly flights. The service is operated by British Airways using a Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner.

events.title: Houthis warned to disrupt Red Sea oil route
events.description: Iran has reportedly instructed Yemen's Houthi movement to be prepared to shut down the Red Sea oil route in response to potential U.S. strikes on Iranian infrastructure, posing a major risk to global shipping.

events.title: Middle East conflict impacts 2026 air cargo rates
events.description: The ongoing war in the Middle East is expected to drive air cargo shipping rates up by 15% by 2026, according to market intelligence firm Xeneta, due to disruptions in freight flows.

events.title: Tanker Seized by Suspected Pirates in Gulf of Aden
events.location: Gulf of Aden, off Yemen coast
events.description: Armed assailants are believed to have boarded the chemical tanker Asana off the southern coast of Yemen in the Gulf of Aden, taking control of the vessel.

events.title: Shipping Threats Expand Across Gulf Region
events.description: INTERTANKO warns that security threats to commercial shipping have expanded beyond the Strait of Hormuz into the broader Gulf region, following recent attacks on merchant vessels.

events.title: Russian drone strikes damage ships at Ukrainian port
events.description: Russian drone strikes damaged foreign-flagged ships at a Ukrainian Black Sea port, killing three people and intensifying pressure on Ukraine's trade routes.

events.title: Iran Demonstrates Capability to Disrupt Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran has shown it can disrupt the Strait of Hormuz using speedboats, missiles, drones, and mines, challenging U.S. naval dominance in the region. This highlights ongoing risks to global oil shipments through this critical maritime chokepoint.

events.title: US and Iran attack each other's infrastructure
events.description: The United States attacked bridges and an airport in Iran, while Iran responded by striking a power and desalination plant in Kuwait, escalating tensions and risking further disruption to regional infrastructure and supply chains.

events.title: US-Iran attacks disrupt Persian Gulf supplies
events.description: US strikes on Iran have intensified, threatening Persian Gulf supplies and causing European natural gas prices to rise to €55.7 per MWh, the highest in nearly four months.

events.title: Tanker attacks disrupt Gulf oil flows
events.description: Renewed attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz are disrupting oil exports, causing the physical oil market to tighten and pushing Brent prices back into the mid-$80s. This reverses an earlier recovery in Persian Gulf flows and introduces fresh upside risks to price forecasts.

events.title: Hormuz Strait shipping disruption impacts LNG flows
events.description: Conflict in the Middle East has disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a key route for liquefied natural gas (LNG), sending shockwaves through global gas markets.

events.title: Black Sea conflict drives feed millers to switch to corn
events.description: Tensions in the Black Sea have increased freight and CFR destination rates, prompting Asian feed millers to substitute wheat with corn in feed formulations due to rising wheat prices.

events.title: Black Sea shipping disruptions impact wheat prices
events.description: Vessel movement disruptions in the Kerch Strait and strikes in the Azov Sea have caused CIF Marmara wheat prices to rebound from a 19-month low, according to market participants.

events.title: QatarEnergy reduces LNG supply to Bangladesh due to Middle East war
events.description: Bangladesh's Petrobangla will receive only half of its planned 2026 LNG deliveries from QatarEnergy due to the war in the Middle East. This follows QatarEnergy's decision to halt LNG production in March.

events.title: New rail corridor boosts automotive logistics in Italy
events.description: Automar, part of the Grimaldi Group, has launched a new rail corridor connecting Livorno, Pontecagnano, and Gioia Tauro in partnership with FS Logistix and others, enhancing automotive logistics between central and southern Italy.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz security crisis impacts regional trade
events.description: A security crisis around the Strait of Hormuz is disrupting trade and prompting changes in port strategies across the Persian Gulf and Indian Ocean regions.

events.title: Sea Legend resumes Arctic route for China-Europe freight
events.description: Sea Legend Shipping is operating eight China-Europe sailings via the Northern Sea Route between August and October, with transit times of 21 days. This reflects a strategic shift in freight routing amid ongoing global supply chain dynamics.

events.title: EU lawmakers push to halt Arctic LNG tanker repairs
events.description: Over 100 European lawmakers have called on Danish shipyard Fayard to cease repairs on Arc7 ice-class tankers used for Russian Arctic LNG exports, targeting the last EU yard servicing the fleet.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Crisis Worsens with Containership Attack
events.description: A 7,000 TEU Global Feeder Shipping containership was attacked and caught fire in the Strait of Hormuz, forcing the crew to abandon ship. The incident has led to a sharp decline in traffic and renewed tensions between the US and Iran over the strait's status.

events.title: Iran urges Houthis to prepare to block Bab el-Mandeb Strait
events.description: Iran is reportedly urging Yemen's Houthi movement to prepare to shut down the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, a critical global shipping chokepoint, in response to potential U.S. attacks on Iranian infrastructure.

events.title: Iranian-linked tankers evade US blockade in Gulf of Oman
events.description: Two US-sanctioned Iranian-linked tankers are zig-zagging and making U-turns in the Gulf of Oman and Arabian Sea to avoid a US-enforced blockade. The vessels are part of a flotilla transporting Iranian energy exports amid heightened tensions.

events.title: EU Pressures Denmark to Stop Servicing Russian Arctic LNG Vessels
events.description: Over 100 European lawmakers have urged Denmark’s Fayard shipyard to cease servicing Russian Arctic LNG carriers, targeting the last European facility maintaining vessels for Moscow’s Yamal LNG project.

events.title: Yemen conflict escalates near Bab el Mandeb chokepoint
events.description: Political tensions in Yemen, exacerbated by extreme summer heat, are bringing the Bab el Mandeb Strait back to the brink of instability, raising concerns about potential disruptions to global shipping through this critical maritime chokepoint.

events.title: COSCO launches Peru-Costa Rica CHX3 service
events.description: COSCO SHIPPING Lines has launched a new weekly container service (CHX3) connecting Peru's Paita and Chancay with Costa Rica's Caldera, enhancing regional supply chain efficiency in Latin America.

events.title: U.S. Marines board falsified-flag tanker in Gulf of Oman
events.description: On July 16, the USS Boxer Amphibious Ready Group intercepted and boarded a 'shadow fleet' VLCC in the Gulf of Oman, part of a broader effort to counter illicit maritime activity.

events.title: Sea of Azov disruptions impact wheat supply
events.description: Renewed geopolitical tensions around the Sea of Azov are contributing to a surge in French wheat prices, as supply chain disruptions and weather risks in the EU further strain grain markets.

events.title: Port of Antwerp-Bruges cargo handling declines 2.4% in H1 2026
events.description: In the first half of 2026, the Port of Antwerp-Bruges handled 133.9 million tonnes of maritime cargo, a 2.4% decline compared to the same period in 2025. The decline is attributed to geopolitical tensions and trade conflicts affecting global freight flows.

events.title: Ukrainian port strikes disrupt grain trade
events.description: Grain traders in Ukraine have reduced local purchases due to intensified port strikes since July 10, with many halting operations as of July 15. Limited new purchases and existing port stocks are reported.

events.title: US-Iran hostilities escalate in Arabian Gulf
events.description: The US Central Command has launched strikes on Iranian sites along the southern coast, and disabled an Iran-linked oil tanker in the Arabian Gulf, escalating tensions between the US and Iran.

events.title: Shipping firms avoid Hormuz amid U.S.-Iran clashes
events.description: Global shipping companies are avoiding the Strait of Hormuz due to escalating U.S.-Iran tensions and a lack of trust in U.S. escort plans. The situation has increased risk for maritime transit through this key chokepoint.

events.title: Panamax congestion at Chinese ports
events.description: A surge in seaborne coal imports and typhoon-related delays have caused a growing queue of laden Panamax vessels waiting to berth in southern China.

events.title: Hormuz Strikes Disrupt Tanker Market
events.description: Recent strikes in the Middle East have caused the tanker market to return to a 'wartime pattern' of behavior, disrupting freight flows through the critical Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Hormuz tensions disrupt Persian Gulf crude flows
events.description: Renewed tensions at the Strait of Hormuz are causing tanker owners to consider shifting Persian Gulf crude flows to the US Gulf Coast. While activity remains limited, the situation is prompting concerns over potential disruptions to regional shipping.

events.title: Churchill Port resumes grain exports after six-year hiatus
events.description: Canada’s Port of Churchill is set to export grain for the first time in six years, signaling a revival of operations at the Arctic port. The move reflects efforts to expand and revitalize the facility’s role in the supply chain.

events.title: Ukraine reports 147 Russian shipping strikes
events.description: Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces struck 11 additional civilian vessels in Russian-controlled waters on July 16, raising the total to 147. The attacks disrupt shipping and heighten tensions in the Black Sea region.

events.title: GT Lines upgrades KIX 1 service to weekly frequency
events.description: GT Lines, part of Gulftainer, has upgraded its Khorfakkan India Express (KIX 1) service to a weekly schedule, enhancing connections between West India and the Gulf. The service began on 15 July 2026, calling at Nhava Sheva, Mundra, and Khorfakkan with vessels of 1,100-1,400 TEUs.

events.title: India Bans Seafarers from Hormuz Strait Transits
events.description: India has banned its seafarers from transiting the Strait of Hormuz following recent attacks on two ships on July 14, which resulted in one death and nine injuries.

events.title: Greece Blocks EU Sanctions on Yamal LNG
events.description: Greece has blocked the latest EU sanctions package on Russian energy exports, including Yamal LNG, to protect its domestic shipping interests. This move could disrupt EU efforts to isolate Russian energy and affect LNG freight flows.

events.title: US-Iran tensions threaten energy corridors
events.description: The United States launched fresh strikes against Iran, escalating tensions that could disrupt global energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for oil shipments.

events.title: Maersk adds low water surcharge on Europe-Canada trade
events.description: Maersk is introducing a Low Water Surcharge (LWC) on shipments from Europe to Montreal and Halifax, Canada, effective 1 August 2026. The surcharge applies to cargo moving to or via these ports, with rates set at US$150 per 20ft container and US$300 per 40ft container.

events.title: Ukraine strikes Russia-linked oil tankers in Black Sea
events.description: Ukrainian naval drones hit two large Russia-linked oil tankers in the Black Sea, expanding maritime conflict between Moscow and Kyiv and potentially disrupting regional shipping and energy flows.

events.title: Port of Antwerp-Bruges cargo volumes drop 2.4% in H1 2026
events.description: The Port of Antwerp-Bruges reported a 2.4% decline in cargo volumes for H1 2026, citing geopolitical tensions, trade conflicts, and operational disruptions as key factors affecting freight flows.

events.title: Maersk adjusts intermodal fuel fees in Europe
events.description: Maersk has updated its Intermodal Fuel Fees across multiple European markets due to ongoing volatility in global energy prices, particularly influenced by the security situation in the Middle East. The changes aim to maintain service continuity amid rising fuel costs.

events.title: ANL adjusts inland fuel surcharge in Australia
events.description: ANL has updated its Inland Emergency Fuel Surcharge in Australia due to ongoing global fuel market volatility. The carrier cited elevated diesel prices and increased inland transportation costs, which may impact supply chain operations.

events.title: US forces disable Iran-bound tanker in Arabian Gulf
events.description: U.S. forces disabled an unladen oil tanker in the Arabian Gulf that was attempting to sail toward Iran, violating a naval blockade. The incident could impact regional shipping and freight flows.

events.title: Ukraine drone strikes hit Russian-linked vessels in Black Sea
events.description: Ukraine reports striking 147 Russian-linked vessels over 11 days in a drone campaign targeting maritime logistics, expanding operations into the Black Sea.

events.title: Iran urges Houthis to block Red Sea oil route
events.description: Iran has instructed Yemen's Houthi movement to prepare to close the Red Sea oil route if the U.S. strikes Iranian power infrastructure, threatening global energy supplies.

events.title: Maersk closes Harlingen inland container depot
events.description: Maersk will close its HOV Harlingen inland container depot in the Netherlands by 3 August 2026 as part of a network optimization. The move aims to streamline inland logistics, with customers redirected to nearby depots in Leeuwarden.

events.title: US imposes 25% tariff on Brazilian imports
events.description: The Trump administration will impose a 25% tariff on certain Brazilian imports starting 22 July, impacting selected goods while exempting those that could disrupt US supply chains.

events.title: Intra-Asia freight rates decline as early peak season ends
events.description: Intra-Asia freight rates have declined, indicating the end of an early peak season, with congestion in Southeast Asian ports beginning to ease. The Shanghai Containerised Freight Index and Drewry's Intra-Asia Container Index both reported rate drops in early July.

events.title: Antwerp Port Quays Reopen After Toxic Gas Leak
events.description: Some quays in Antwerp’s Deurganckdok port area have reopened following a toxic gas leak that hospitalized 155 people. Quay 1718 and parts of Antwerp Gateway are now operational, while Quay 1741 remains closed.

events.title: Floods disrupt Chittagong Port operations
events.description: Heavy rain has caused flooding at Chittagong Port, Bangladesh's main maritime gateway, disrupting cargo movement and delaying vessel sailings. Exporters are demanding compensation for damaged goods and supply chain delays.

events.title: India restricts seafarers from Hormuz voyages
events.description: India has instructed shipowners and recruitment agencies not to deploy Indian seafarers on vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz due to renewed regional conflict, signaling potential disruption to this critical maritime chokepoint.

events.title: US strikes Iranian oil tanker near Hormuz
events.description: The US struck a sanctioned Iranian oil tanker near Iran's main export terminal, escalating tensions and causing a decline in shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Greek Veto Blocks EU Russia Sanctions Affecting Arctic LNG Shipping
events.description: Greece has blocked the EU's 21st sanctions package against Russia, seeking exemptions for its Arctic LNG shipping interests. The delay could impact restrictions on Russian energy exports and affect specialized LNG carriers.

events.title: Russian oil refining at 21-year low due to drone strikes
events.description: Russian oil refining volumes have dropped to a 21-year low at 3.80 million barrels per day due to damage from Ukrainian drone strikes, according to Kpler. The attacks have caused significant refinery downtime and disrupted energy production.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz disruptions impact petroleum markets
events.description: Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz during Q2 2026 caused volatility in crude oil prices and prompted international buyers to seek alternative supply sources for petroleum products.

events.title: US-Iran tensions escalate, impacting oil markets
events.description: Renewed military actions by the US against Iran and potential escalation are causing concern in the physical oil market, with oil prices rising due to fears of disrupted supply flows.

events.title: Black Sea grain corridor under threat from conflict
events.description: Escalating Ukrainian and Russian strikes in the Black Sea are raising concerns over disruptions to a key global grain export corridor. Ukrainian forces have targeted Russian vessels, impacting freight flows.

events.title: South Korea forms labor-management council for shipbuilding workforce gaps
events.description: The South Korean government launched a tripartite labor-management council to address workforce issues in the shipbuilding industry, including skilled worker shortages and job insecurity. The initiative involves the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Resources and the Ministry of Employment and Labor.

events.title: US imposes 25% tariffs on Brazilian imports
events.description: The United States has imposed 25% tariffs on most Brazilian imports following a trade investigation. The move, directed by President Donald Trump, is expected to disrupt trade flows and increase costs for Brazilian exporters.

events.title: China launches weekly Arctic container service
events.description: Sea Legend Shipping is launching a weekly container service via the Northern Sea Route from China to northern Europe, marking a shift in Arctic freight utilization.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Disruption Aftereffects
events.description: Christoph Toepfer warns that disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz may become a recurring issue, affecting global energy trade and tanker operations.

events.title: US military strikes tanker near Iran
events.description: The US military fired missiles at a Curaçao-flagged tanker, the Belma, as it approached Iran's Kharg Island. The vessel was disabled after ignoring warnings, raising concerns over regional tensions and potential disruptions to oil exports.

events.title: Port of Long Beach channel deepening approved
events.description: A key congressional committee approved a $255 million project to deepen the main shipping channel at the Port of Long Beach, aimed at accommodating larger vessels and improving cargo flows.
